MAMMOGRAPHY OF BOTH BREASTS,05/06/2017,62,This is a screening mammogram.

Mammograms of both breasts were obtained using low radiation film screen technique. Markers are put in outer and axillary sides of the film.

The breasts are almost entirely fatty.
(Type a parenchyma).

Bilateral axillary nodes.

No focal spiculated mass, architectural distortion or micro calcifications noted on either side.

Skin and nipple areolar complex are normal on both sides.




,Type a parenchyma.
Bilateral axillary node.
BIRADS (ACR) Category 2- Benign.
,DR.KULKARNI ASHWINI

24/08/2024 15:24,24/08/2024,
Type a parenchyma.
Left axillary nodes.
Benign calcification seen in right breast.
No suspicious lesion seen on these mammograms.
BIRADS (ACR) Category 2- Benign.,Dr. CMO .,Clinical History:-This is a screening mammogram.

Technique:- Fullfield digital mammography with combination of 3D tomosynthesis. Images were reviewed at 1 mm interval.

Density:- The breasts are almost entirely fatty.(Type a parenchyma).

Findings:-
Left axillary nodes are seen.
Benign calcification seen in right breast.
No focal spiculated mass, architectural distortion or micro calcifications noted on either side.
No focal lesion or parenchymal distortion is seen in both breasts on tomosynthesis.
Skin and nipple areolar complex are normal on both sides.,DIGITAL MAMMOGRAPHY,Dr. JOSHI DEVASHREE,DR.JOSHI DEVASHREE
